title: Waymo Suspends Service in Six Cities After Cars Drove Into Flooded Roads
description: The temporary shutdowns came after videos emerged showing two Waymo cars stopped on swamped streets in Atlanta on Wednesday.
author: Michael Levenson

title: Google Appeals Landmark Ruling Declaring It a Monopolist in Search
description: Google asked a federal appeals court on Friday to reverse a judge’s ruling that it had violated antitrust laws, as well as a requirement it share some data with rivals.
author: David McCabe

title: Sundar Pichai Understands Why People Are Anxious About A.I.
description: After a busy Google I/O, the company’s chief executive sits down with the hosts of “Hard Fork” to discuss the future of Google Search, how he’s using A.I. agents and his advice for college graduates.
author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton and Whitney Jones

title: Our Field Trip to Google I/O + A Sit-Down With Sundar Pichai + System Update
description: “This is the only recent gathering of a large number of people where mentions of A.I. did not produce a large chorus of boos.”
author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Rachel Cohn, Whitney Jones, Vjeran Pavic, Katie McMurran, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Rowan Niemisto and Alyssa Moxley

title: ‘Peanuts’ Music Owner Sues Interior Department and 3 Companies for Copyright Infringement
description: The owner of the “Peanuts” catalog would really like it if companies and the U.S. government stopped using its music without permission.
author: Aimee Ortiz

title: Meta Settles a School District’s Social Media Addiction Lawsuit
description: Snap, TikTok and YouTube had already settled with the Kentucky district, allowing the companies to avert the first in a series of federal trials.
author: Cecilia Kang

title: Trump Cancels Signing of Executive Order Granting Oversight of A.I. Models
description: The president said he postponed the executive order, which would give the government power to evaluate A.I. models before their release, over concerns about “aspects of it.”
author: Tripp Mickle and Sheera Frenkel

title: Gov. Gavin Newsom to Sign Executive Order Aimed at A.I. Job Loss
description: Gov. Gavin Newsom issued an executive order to explore an overhaul of labor policies to deal with potential mass job displacement from artificial intelligence.
author: Cecilia Kang

title: Trump Approved a Nvidia Chip for Sale in China. Beijing Doesn’t Want It.
description: The U.S. tech giant’s powerful H200 chip seemed poised to boost Beijing’s A.I. ambitions, but not a single one has been purchased in China.
author: Meaghan Tobin and Tripp Mickle

title: Samsung Averts a Walkout With Big Bonuses, but Discord Over A.I. Profits Brews
description: Samsung Electronics, a global memory chip supplier, has been a major beneficiary of the A.I boom. It has also become the center of a debate over how to divide its spoils.
author: Max Kim

title: Bluesky Says Kremlin Is Hacking Its Platform to Spread Propaganda
description: The company said it was fighting Russian efforts to hijack real users’ accounts to post fake content, an apparently novel tactic.
author: Steven Lee Myers

title: Soundtrack to 8,000 Job Cuts: A Meta Worker’s Layoff-Themed A.I. Songs
description: On a dark day of layoffs at Meta, one employee responded by creating an internal radio station that plays songs about job cuts — generated by artificial intelligence, of course.
author: Mike Isaac

title: Nvidia’s Profit Hits $58.3 Billion as A.I. Boom Gathers More Steam
description: The chip maker said its profit in its most recent quarter jumped 211 percent from a year earlier thanks to extreme demand from other big technology companies.
author: Tripp Mickle

title: Musk’s SpaceX Reveals Its Finances for the First Time as It Readies for IPO
description: Mr. Musk’s rocket and satellite maker disclosed its financial performance as it prepares to go public in what is set to be one of the largest offerings to date.
author: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch

title: OpenAI Prepares to File for an I.P.O. in Coming Weeks
description: OpenAI would be one of the most highly anticipated potential initial public offerings, in what is set to be a major year of I.P.O.s for Silicon Valley.
author: Mike Isaac and Cade Metz

title: Peter G. Neumann, Who Warned of Computer Security Risks, Dies at 93
description: For decades, he criticized the industry’s lax attitudes toward computer security and individual digital privacy. He also developed solutions.
author: John Markoff

title: Here’s what was on the jury’s list for a decision.
author: Natallie Rocha

title: What to Know About Elon Musk’s Trial Against OpenAI
description: A jury in Oakland, Calif., reached a decision after a three-week-long trial seen as pivotal for the future of OpenAI and the artificial intelligence race.
author: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
